workflowInfo.workflowDescription: Reports spool utilization, largest consumers by job and owner, and a verdict

variable.name: SPOOL_HIGH_WATER
variable.label: High Water Threshold
variable.abstract: Percent used that should trigger urgent housekeeping
variable.description: Numeric percentage. Overall spool utilization at or above this value produces an ABOVE THRESHOLD verdict.

variable.name: TOP_N
variable.label: Top Job Count
variable.abstract: How many largest spool consumers to report
variable.description: Whole number. Controls how many jobs and how many owners are listed in the consumer report.

step.name: spoolUtilization
step.title: Report Spool Utilization
step.description: Reads the SDSF SP (spool volumes) panel through SDSF/REXX and reports per-volume and overall spool utilization, then judges it against the high water threshold.
step.instructions:
This step reports how full the spool actually is.
It reads the SDSF SP panel for every spool volume, totals the track groups used against the track groups available, and prints the overall utilization percentage. It then compares that percentage to the ${instance-SPOOL_HIGH_WATER}% threshold and prints a verdict: ABOVE THRESHOLD (housekeeping required, with a recommended order of work) or BELOW THRESHOLD (monitor only).
The step fails if SDSF cannot supply the TGUSE column, which happens when another SDSF panel has already been used in the same TSO address space. If that occurs, run this step in a fresh TSO session.

template.inlineTemplate:
/* REXX - Spool utilization from the SDSF SP panel. */ /* ONE SDSF panel per TSO address space: this exec touches SP only. */ /* Using a second panel here would cut SDSF down to a 6-column set */ /* for the rest of the address space (TGUSE would disappear). */ SIGNAL ON NOVALUE NAME HANDLE_ERROR
HIGHWM = '${instance-SPOOL_HIGH_WATER}'
IF DATATYPE(HIGHWM,'N') = 0 THEN DO SAY 'SPOOL_HIGH_WATER is not numeric:' HIGHWM EXIT 12 END
X = ISFCALLS('ON') IF X <> 0 THEN DO SAY 'ISFCALLS ON failed, RC=' X EXIT 12 END
/* Stem defaults: SDSF leaves a variable UNSET for a row that has no */ /* value for that field, so an unguarded reference would trap. */ DEVNAME. = '' STATUS. = '' TGNUM. = 0 TGUSE. = 0 TGPCT. = 0
ADDRESS SDSF "ISFEXEC SP" IF RC <> 0 THEN DO SAY 'ISFEXEC SP failed, RC=' RC CALL BAIL 12 END
IF WORDPOS('TGUSE',ISFCOLS) = 0 THEN DO SAY 'ERROR: SDSF returned a reduced column set (', WORDS(ISFCOLS) 'columns); TGUSE is missing.' SAY 'Cause: another SDSF panel was already used in this TSO address' SAY 'space, which permanently reduces the column set. Run this step' SAY 'in a fresh TSO session.' CALL BAIL 8 END
SAY COPIES('=',72) SAY 'SPOOL UTILIZATION -' DATE('S') TIME() SAY COPIES('=',72) SAY LEFT('VOLUME',9) LEFT('STATUS',9) RIGHT('TG TOTAL',9), RIGHT('TG USED',9) RIGHT('PCT',7) SAY COPIES('-',72)
TOTTG = 0 USETG = 0 DO I = 1 TO DEVNAME.0 TOTTG = TOTTG + TGNUM.I USETG = USETG + TGUSE.I SAY LEFT(DEVNAME.I,9) LEFT(STATUS.I,9) RIGHT(TGNUM.I,9), RIGHT(TGUSE.I,9) RIGHT(TGPCT.I,6)'%' END
IF TOTTG = 0 THEN DO SAY 'No spool volumes reported track groups - nothing to measure.' CALL BAIL 8 END
PCT = FORMAT(USETG * 100 / TOTTG,,2) SAY COPIES('-',72) SAY 'Volumes:' DEVNAME.0 ' Track groups used:' USETG 'of' TOTTG SAY 'OVERALL SPOOL UTILIZATION:' PCT'% Threshold:' HIGHWM'%' SAY COPIES('=',72)
IF PCT >= HIGHWM THEN DO SAY 'VERDICT: ABOVE THRESHOLD -' PCT'% >=' HIGHWM'%' SAY 'Housekeeping is REQUIRED. Recommended order:' SAY ' 1. Purge obsolete held output (QUEUE=PRINT) for the top owners.' SAY ' 2. Confirm top jobs are not needed for audit or problem' SAY ' determination before purging.' SAY ' 3. If one job dominates, correct the application or stop the' SAY ' workload - purging alone will not hold.' SAY ' 4. Offload dumps and large reports, then purge the spool copy.' SAY ' 5. Escalate to JES operations if pressure persists.' END ELSE DO SAY 'VERDICT: BELOW THRESHOLD -' PCT'% <' HIGHWM'%' SAY 'No urgent action. Trend the top owners from the next step.' END SAY COPIES('=',72) SAY 'SPOOL UTILIZATION COMPLETE'
CALL BAIL 0
BAIL: X = ISFCALLS('OFF') EXIT ARG(1)
HANDLE_ERROR: SAY 'REXX NOVALUE at line' SIGL 'variable:' CONDITION('D') EXIT 12

step.name: topConsumers
step.title: Report Largest Spool Consumers
step.description: Reads the SDSF ST panel through SDSF/REXX and reports the largest jobs by track groups held, plus the same ranking aggregated by owner.
step.instructions:
This step reports who is actually holding the spool.
It reads the SDSF ST panel for every job on the spool and prints two tables: the ${instance-TOP_N} largest jobs by track groups held (with job ID, owner and queue), and the ${instance-TOP_N} largest owners with their job counts and share of job-held spool.
SHARE% is each owner's share of the spool currently held by jobs, so it is a different denominator from the overall utilization in the previous step. The two are not meant to add up: overall utilization also includes spool that is not held by jobs.
This step runs SDSF in its own TSO address space and deliberately does not touch the SP panel. It fails if SDSF cannot supply the OWNERID column, which happens when another SDSF panel has already been used in the same TSO address space.

template.inlineTemplate:
/* REXX - Largest spool consumers from the SDSF ST panel. */ /* ONE SDSF panel per TSO address space: this exec touches ST only. */ /* Adding an SP call here would cut SDSF down to a 6-column set and */ /* OWNERID / JOBID / QUEUE would come back empty. */ /* Owner share is computed from TGNUM (track groups) against the */ /* spool held by all jobs. Do NOT sum the per-job TGPCT column to */ /* get it: TGPCT is rounded to 2dp, so the hundreds of small jobs */ /* each round to 0.00% and the total silently loses ~8 points. */ SIGNAL ON NOVALUE NAME HANDLE_ERROR
TOPN = '${instance-TOP_N}'
IF DATATYPE(TOPN,'W') = 0 THEN DO SAY 'TOP_N is not a whole number:' TOPN EXIT 12 END IF TOPN < 1 THEN DO SAY 'TOP_N must be 1 or more:' TOPN EXIT 12 END
X = ISFCALLS('ON') IF X <> 0 THEN DO SAY 'ISFCALLS ON failed, RC=' X EXIT 12 END
ISFPREFIX = '*' ISFOWNER = '*'
/* Stem defaults: SDSF leaves a variable UNSET for a row that has no */ /* value for that field (e.g. a job with no JOBID yet), so an */ /* unguarded reference would trap. */ JNAME. = '' JOBID. = '' OWNERID. = '' QUEUE. = '' TGNUM. = 0 TGPCT. = 0
ADDRESS SDSF "ISFEXEC ST" IF RC <> 0 THEN DO SAY 'ISFEXEC ST failed, RC=' RC CALL BAIL 12 END
IF WORDPOS('OWNERID',ISFCOLS) = 0 THEN DO SAY 'ERROR: SDSF returned a reduced column set (', WORDS(ISFCOLS) 'columns); OWNERID is missing.' SAY 'Cause: another SDSF panel was already used in this TSO address' SAY 'space, which permanently reduces the column set. Run this step' SAY 'in a fresh TSO session.' CALL BAIL 8 END
N = JNAME.0 SAY COPIES('=',72) SAY 'LARGEST SPOOL CONSUMERS -' DATE('S') TIME() SAY 'Jobs on spool:' N SAY COPIES('=',72)
/* ---- top N jobs by track groups held ---------------------------- */ SAY 'TOP' TOPN 'JOBS BY SPOOL USAGE' SAY COPIES('-',72) SAY LEFT('JOBNAME',9) LEFT('JOBID',9) LEFT('OWNER',9) LEFT('QUEUE',10), RIGHT('TRK GRP',8) RIGHT('SPOOL%',7) SAY COPIES('-',72)
USED. = 0 DO K = 1 TO TOPN BEST = -1 BESTI = 0 DO I = 1 TO N IF USED.I = 1 THEN ITERATE IF TGNUM.I > BEST THEN DO BEST = TGNUM.I BESTI = I END END IF BESTI = 0 THEN LEAVE USED.BESTI = 1 IF TGNUM.BESTI = 0 THEN LEAVE /* rest hold no spool */ OWN = OWNERID.BESTI IF OWN = '' THEN OWN = '(none)' JID = JOBID.BESTI IF JID = '' THEN JID = '(none)' SAY LEFT(JNAME.BESTI,9) LEFT(JID,9) LEFT(OWN,9), LEFT(QUEUE.BESTI,10) RIGHT(TGNUM.BESTI,8) RIGHT(TGPCT.BESTI,6)'%' END
/* ---- aggregate by owner ----------------------------------------- */ OWNTG. = 0 OWNJOB. = 0 SEEN. = 0 OWNLIST = '' JOBTG = 0 DO I = 1 TO N O = OWNERID.I IF O = '' THEN O = '(none)' IF SEEN.O = 0 THEN DO SEEN.O = 1 OWNLIST = OWNLIST O END OWNTG.O = OWNTG.O + TGNUM.I OWNJOB.O = OWNJOB.O + 1 JOBTG = JOBTG + TGNUM.I END
SAY ' ' SAY 'TOP' TOPN 'OWNERS BY SPOOL USAGE (distinct owners:', WORDS(OWNLIST)')' SAY 'SHARE% = share of the spool currently held by jobs.' SAY COPIES('-',72) SAY LEFT('OWNER',9) RIGHT('JOBS',6) RIGHT('TRK GRP',9), RIGHT('SHARE%',8) SAY COPIES('-',72)
DONE. = 0 DO K = 1 TO TOPN BEST = -1 BESTO = '' DO J = 1 TO WORDS(OWNLIST) O = WORD(OWNLIST,J) IF DONE.O = 1 THEN ITERATE IF OWNTG.O > BEST THEN DO BEST = OWNTG.O BESTO = O END END IF BESTO = '' THEN LEAVE DONE.BESTO = 1 IF OWNTG.BESTO = 0 THEN LEAVE SHARE = 0 IF JOBTG > 0 THEN SHARE = FORMAT(OWNTG.BESTO * 100 / JOBTG,,2) SAY LEFT(BESTO,9) RIGHT(OWNJOB.BESTO,6) RIGHT(OWNTG.BESTO,9), RIGHT(SHARE,7)'%' END
SAY COPIES('-',72) SAY 'Track groups held by jobs:' JOBTG '(100% of SHARE% above)' SAY 'Overall spool utilization, including non-job use, is reported by' SAY 'the spool utilization step - these two steps use different' SAY 'denominators and are not meant to add up.' SAY COPIES('=',72) SAY 'CONSUMER REPORT COMPLETE'
CALL BAIL 0
BAIL: X = ISFCALLS('OFF') EXIT ARG(1)
HANDLE_ERROR: SAY 'REXX NOVALUE at line' SIGL 'variable:' CONDITION('D') EXIT 12

step.name: reviewFindings
step.title: Review Utilization and Largest Owners
step.description: Guides the operator through interpreting the two reports before any spool is purged.
step.instructions:
Review the output of the two previous steps together.
Confirm these items:
- The overall utilization percentage, and whether it reached the ${instance-SPOOL_HIGH_WATER}% threshold
- Whether any single volume is far more full than the others, which points at a volume problem rather than a workload problem
- Which owners dominate the SHARE% column, and whether they are known batch windows or unexpected workloads
- Whether the largest jobs sit in EXECUTION or PRINT: jobs in EXECUTION are still running and cannot simply be purged
If the spool is under pressure, classify the largest consumers as:
- Long-running started tasks with excessive SYSOUT
- Held output not yet printed or purged
- Failed batch jobs generating large dumps or reports
- Repeating jobs creating duplicate output classes
Record the top contributors before purging anything, so growth can be trended across runs.

step.name: housekeepingChecklist
step.title: Print Housekeeping Sign-Off Checklist
step.description: Prints a fixed operator sign-off checklist to work through after the reports have been reviewed. The checklist is the same every run; the data-driven verdict is in the utilization step.
step.instructions:
Prints a standard sign-off checklist for the housekeeping action.
This checklist is fixed text and does not change with the measured spool figures. The verdict and the recommended order of work come from the utilization step; this step is the record of what was done.

template.inlineTemplate: #!/bin/sh echo "SPOOL HOUSEKEEPING SIGN-OFF CHECKLIST" echo "=====================================" echo echo "Threshold configured : ${instance-SPOOL_HIGH_WATER}%" echo "Consumers listed : ${instance-TOP_N}" echo echo "Before purging:" echo "- Top contributors recorded so growth can be trended across runs." echo "- Largest jobs checked for ACTIVE, OUTPUT or HELD state." echo "- Owners matched against known batch windows." echo "- Output confirmed as not needed for audit, print, or problem" echo " determination." echo "- Dumps and large reports offloaded before their spool copy goes." echo echo "After purging:" echo "- Utilization step re-run to confirm the reclaim actually landed." echo "- Any job creating abnormal output growth raised with its owner," echo " since purging alone will not hold." echo "- JES operations engaged if pressure persists after cleanup." echo echo "CHECKLIST COMPLETE"
